description: Current prevailing asking price per square foot in the surrounding micromarket radius.
why_it_matters: This is the baseline from which the fair price engine derives all adjustments.
help_text: Use the 3 km radius average from your data source.
tooltip_text: The micromarket benchmark drives the baseline fair price.
output_impact: All adjustments are expressed relative to this value.

description: Current stage of the project in its development lifecycle.
why_it_matters: Stage determines construction-risk discount applied by the engine.
help_text: Choose the stage that reflects current ground status.
tooltip_text: Ready-to-move projects carry zero construction risk.
output_impact: Affects construction risk component of fair price.

description: Registration status under the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act.
why_it_matters: Registered projects carry stronger legal protection, reducing buyer risk.
help_text: Unregistered status materially increases risk discount.
tooltip_text: Unregistered projects carry a risk penalty in the engine.
output_impact: Non-registered status applies a legal risk discount.

description: Total number of residential units in the project.
why_it_matters: Scale impacts liquidity and developer's ability to sustain premium.
help_text: Use the approved RERA unit count.
tooltip_text: Larger projects may show different absorption patterns.
output_impact: Scale can influence confidence and absorption assumptions.

description: Average carpet or built-up area of a typical unit in the project.
why_it_matters: Unit size relative to the market average affects ticket size and demand.
help_text: Use carpet area if RERA-registered; built-up area otherwise.
tooltip_text: Larger units carry higher total ticket size and different demand profile.
output_impact: Affects product sizing premium/discount.

description: Composite score representing project amenities, clubhouse, open areas, and lifestyle offering.
why_it_matters: Projects with stronger amenity quality often sustain better premiums in the same micromarket.
help_text: Use a 0 to 10 scale.
tooltip_text: Higher amenity score typically supports a product premium.
output_impact: Higher score can improve current fair price and price stickiness.

description: Assessment of materials, fittings, external facade, and build quality.
why_it_matters: Build quality differentiates projects in the same micromarket and sustains resale value.
help_text: Use 0 to 10 scale based on site visit or peer review.
tooltip_text: Higher quality scores typically add a product premium.
output_impact: Higher quality supports both current price and long-term projection.

description: Score reflecting title clarity, litigation risk, and encumbrance status.
why_it_matters: Legal uncertainty significantly increases buyer risk and suppresses price.
help_text: Use 0 to 10. Score of 10 = fully clear title, no litigation.
tooltip_text: Low legal clarity can penalise price despite strong location.
output_impact: Low scores reduce fair price and reduce confidence rating.

description: Percentage of total project area dedicated to open green and recreational space.
why_it_matters: Higher open space improves livability and supports premium pricing.
help_text: Use the figure from RERA or project brochure.
tooltip_text: RERA mandates minimum open space. Premium above 50% benefits pricing.
output_impact: Higher open space positively adjusts specification premium.

description: Average number of parking slots per residential unit.
why_it_matters: Parking adequacy is a growing concern in dense urban markets.
help_text: 1.0 = 1 slot per unit. Values above 1.5 typically command a small premium.
tooltip_text: Higher parking ratio adds marginal premium in denser markets.
output_impact: Parking above 1.0 provides a marginal uplift in specification premium.

description: Number of residential units per acre of total project land.
why_it_matters: Overcrowded projects above 75 units/acre typically face density discounts.
help_text: Compute from total units divided by total land area in acres.
tooltip_text: Lower density typically supports a premium; high density can depress price.
output_impact: Density above 75 units/acre applies a specification penalty.

description: Radius in km used to derive the benchmark price from comparable projects.
why_it_matters: Wider radius introduces more heterogeneous comparables and reduces precision.
help_text: Recommended range: 1–5 km. Use 2–3 km for urban areas.
tooltip_text: Smaller radius gives a more accurate local benchmark.
output_impact: Smaller radius improves confidence score.

description: Average secondary market / resale price per sq ft for comparable projects in the radius.
why_it_matters: Sold prices anchor buyer expectations and affect pricing power vs new launches.
help_text: Use registered transaction data where available.
tooltip_text: If sold prices >> asking, demand is absorbing new supply faster than expected.
output_impact: Large gap between sold and asking prices affects market adjustment factor.

description: Prevailing monthly rental for a comparable unit in the micromarket.
why_it_matters: Rental yield contextualises whether the asking price is justified by income potential.
help_text: Use 2 BHK / 3 BHK average for the locality.
tooltip_text: Gross yield = (annual rent / price). Implied yield below 1.5% signals overpricing.
output_impact: Implied gross yield feeds the market pricing signal factor.

description: Estimated number of months required to absorb current supply at the prevailing sales pace.
why_it_matters: High overhang usually indicates supply pressure and may suppress pricing power.
help_text: Use market-level or micromarket-level estimated months.
tooltip_text: Higher overhang generally increases supply drag.
formula_dependency: Supply pressure discount, future projection drag
output_impact: Higher values usually reduce near-term growth assumptions.

description: Percentage of newly launched units sold within the first 6 months of launch.
why_it_matters: High absorption signals strong demand, supporting pricing power.
help_text: Use micromarket-level average over the last 4 quarters.
tooltip_text: Absorption above 60% in 6 months signals a strong demand market.
output_impact: Higher absorption rate can positively affect near-term growth projection.

description: Total residential units expected to be launched in the micromarket over the next 12 months.
why_it_matters: Large upcoming supply can suppress price appreciation in the medium term.
help_text: Use industry reports or RERA pipeline data for the micromarket.
tooltip_text: Upcoming supply relative to absorption determines net market pressure.
output_impact: High new supply relative to absorption can reduce pricing power.

description: Composite brand perception score of the developer on a 0–10 scale.
why_it_matters: Premium developers command a consistent price premium vs. unknown builders.
help_text: Use your internal developer score or industry ranking.
tooltip_text: Score above 8 typically adds a developer premium of 5–15%.
output_impact: Higher brand score increases the developer premium factor.

description: Historical on-time delivery track record score for the developer.
why_it_matters: Delivery delays increase construction risk and erode buyer confidence.
help_text: Score of 10 = never delayed. Score below 5 = frequent delays.
tooltip_text: Low delivery score increases construction risk discount.
output_impact: Low delivery scores can reduce developer premium or add a risk discount.

description: Score reflecting known litigation, regulatory disputes, or consumer forum cases.
why_it_matters: High litigation risk increases legal uncertainty and depresses secondary demand.
help_text: Higher score = higher litigation risk. Score of 0 = no known litigation.
tooltip_text: Litigation risk above 5 can add a material legal risk discount.
output_impact: High litigation score applies a risk penalty to fair price.

description: Current Reserve Bank of India repo rate, which drives home loan interest rates.
why_it_matters: Higher repo rates raise EMIs, reducing affordability and suppressing demand.
help_text: Enter the current effective repo rate as a percentage.
tooltip_text: Each 50 bps increase in repo rate can suppress demand by 3–5%.
output_impact: Higher rates increase affordability drag on fair price.

description: Current annualised Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation rate.
why_it_matters: High inflation erodes real income and purchasing power, affecting demand.
help_text: Use the headline CPI print from the latest RBI / MOSPI release.
tooltip_text: Real returns = nominal price appreciation minus inflation.
output_impact: High inflation moderately suppresses real demand growth.

description: Expected annual GDP growth rate for the current / next fiscal year.
why_it_matters: GDP growth drives income growth, job creation, and overall real estate demand.
help_text: Use RBI or IMF GDP forecast for India.
tooltip_text: Higher GDP growth supports demand growth assumptions.
output_impact: Higher GDP growth positively adjusts the macro factor in fair price.

description: Road distance from the project to the nearest operational or upcoming metro station.
why_it_matters: Transit access can materially improve demand, liquidity, and pricing power.
help_text: Use realistic road distance, not straight-line distance.
tooltip_text: Lower distance generally strengthens the connectivity premium.
output_impact: Lower distance usually increases fair price and future projection.

description: Road distance from the project to the nearest domestic / international airport.
why_it_matters: Airport proximity adds lifestyle premium for luxury and senior living projects.
help_text: Use current road distance via primary highway.
tooltip_text: Strong for luxury; less relevant for mid-income segments.
output_impact: Close proximity adds to connectivity premium, especially for premium segments.

description: Road distance from the project to the nearest major business district or IT employment hub.
why_it_matters: Proximity to employment strongly drives demand and price in residential markets.
help_text: Use the most relevant employment hub for the project's target buyer segment.
tooltip_text: Projects within 10 km of IT / CBD hubs typically show stronger demand.
output_impact: Closer distance to employment hub increases location premium.

description: Composite score for schools, hospitals, retail, and recreational facilities within 3 km.
why_it_matters: Strong social infrastructure improves livability and sustained demand.
help_text: Score of 10 = excellent schools, hospitals, malls all within 3 km.
tooltip_text: Strong social infra typically supports resale demand.
output_impact: Higher score adds to the location premium factor.

description: Estimated percentage price uplift from planned or under-construction infrastructure projects nearby.
why_it_matters: Infrastructure announcements can significantly increase future value.
help_text: Use 0 if no nearby infra projects. Typical range: 0–15%.
tooltip_text: Metro extension, expressway completion, or DMIC node can add 3–12%.
output_impact: Positive infra uplift increases fair price and 3Y/5Y projections.

description: The macro growth scenario driving forward projection assumptions.
why_it_matters: Scenario choice sets the CAGR and adjustment factors used for all projections.
help_text: Bear = 4% CAGR, Base = 8% CAGR, Bull = 12% CAGR.
tooltip_text: Base scenario reflects consensus market growth expectations.
output_impact: Scenario drives 1Y, 3Y, 5Y price projections.

description: User-supplied annual CAGR to use when 'custom' scenario is selected.
why_it_matters: Allows analysts to model growth assumptions outside the standard three scenarios.
help_text: Only used when Growth scenario is set to 'custom'.
tooltip_text: Enter your own CAGR. Leave blank if using bear/base/bull scenario.
output_impact: Replaces scenario CAGR in all projection calculations.
